name: pending-stub
description: Advance E15 HA validation chain (kind-smoke.sh scaffold and kind runtime tests for chaos-mesh and wiremock). Clear accumulated review debt: RESTRUCTURE-001b-2, HA-008, DEVEX-009, and the CEREMONY-008 SMART guidance improvement.
sprintGoal: Deliver the kind smoke test scaffold (HA-005a) and two kind runtime tests (TEST-004b, TEST-005b) while closing four pending review confirmations from the E2/E15/E11/E1 backlog.

stories.title: platform/deploy.sh: Helm deployment orchestrator with --help, --chart-dir, --namespace, and helm upgrade --install invocation
stories.description: Create platform/deploy.sh. The script must: (1) support --help flag that prints usage including '--chart-dir DIR' and '--namespace NS'; (2) accept --chart-dir DIR and --namespace NS required options; (3) print 'Deploying chart: $chart_dir to namespace: $namespace' before running; (4) invoke 'helm upgrade --install' for the given chart and namespace. Script must pass shellcheck -S error with set -euo pipefail at the top.
acceptanceCriteria: platform/deploy.sh exists: ls platform/deploy.sh exits 0
shellcheck -S error platform/deploy.sh exits 0
bash -n platform/deploy.sh exits 0
bash platform/deploy.sh --help 2>&1 | grep -q 'chart-dir' exits 0
grep -q 'helm upgrade --install' platform/deploy.sh exits 0 (script contains the helm invocation)
stories.testPlan: ls platform/deploy.sh && shellcheck -S error platform/deploy.sh && bash -n platform/deploy.sh && bash platform/deploy.sh --help 2>&1 | grep 'chart-dir' && grep 'helm upgrade --install' platform/deploy.sh && echo 'all checks passed'; show all output verbatim

stories.title: Create scripts/test/kind-smoke.sh: static-verifiable scaffold covering PLATFORM-001 through PLATFORM-004
stories.description: Create scripts/test/kind-smoke.sh that encodes all PLATFORM-001 through PLATFORM-004 acceptance criteria as executable test functions (cert-manager, sealed-secrets, Harbor, Keycloak). The script must define one test function per PLATFORM story, print a PASS/FAIL summary table, and pass shellcheck -S error with no warnings. The exact commands for each test come from the acceptance criteria in prd/increment-21-platform-foundations.json.
acceptanceCriteria: shellcheck -S error scripts/test/kind-smoke.sh exits 0
grep -c 'helm\|kubectl' scripts/test/kind-smoke.sh returns a value >= 4 (at least one invocation per PLATFORM-00x test)
grep -E 'PASS|FAIL' scripts/test/kind-smoke.sh exits 0 (PASS and FAIL literals appear in summary output logic)
stories.testPlan: shellcheck -S error scripts/test/kind-smoke.sh; grep -c 'helm\|kubectl' scripts/test/kind-smoke.sh; grep -E 'PASS|FAIL' scripts/test/kind-smoke.sh; show all output verbatim.
stories.readinessNote: UNBLOCKED: dependency HA-004 has status:complete (delivered in sprint 32 per increment-32-pending-stub.json). No remaining blocks. READY.

stories.title: Chaos Mesh PodChaos experiment YAML + README validates PDB prevents simultaneous pod eviction
stories.description: Create test/chaos/pdb-validation.yaml: a Chaos Mesh PodChaos experiment targeting a platform service (e.g. grafana namespace, mode: all) to simultaneously terminate all pods. The PDB minAvailable:1 should prevent the last pod from being evicted. Create test/chaos/README.md explaining how to apply the experiment against a kind cluster running Chaos Mesh (TEST-004), what PDB-enforcement behavior to observe, and how to clean up. The YAML is a static test artifact — it does not run in CI. This covers the 'PDB chaos validation' goal from E15.
acceptanceCriteria: test/chaos/pdb-validation.yaml exists — ls test/chaos/pdb-validation.yaml exits 0
python3 -c "import yaml, sys; yaml.safe_load(open(sys.argv[1]).read())" test/chaos/pdb-validation.yaml exits 0 (valid YAML)
grep 'kind: PodChaos' test/chaos/pdb-validation.yaml exits 0
grep 'mode: all' test/chaos/pdb-validation.yaml exits 0 (targets all replicas to exercise PDB)
test/chaos/README.md exists and grep -i 'PodDisruptionBudget\|PDB' test/chaos/README.md exits 0
stories.testPlan: ls test/chaos/pdb-validation.yaml test/chaos/README.md && python3 -c "import yaml, sys; yaml.safe_load(open(sys.argv[1]).read())" test/chaos/pdb-validation.yaml && grep 'kind: PodChaos' test/chaos/pdb-validation.yaml && grep 'mode: all' test/chaos/pdb-validation.yaml && grep -i 'PDB' test/chaos/README.md; show all output verbatim

stories.title: Deploy platform/charts/chaos-mesh/ to kind-sovereign-test and run a pod failure experiment
stories.description: Install platform/charts/chaos-mesh/ into kind-sovereign-test in namespace chaos-testing. Create kind/fixtures/podchaos-test.yaml PodChaos experiment fixture targeting a test pod. Apply the fixture and verify the experiment reaches phase=Finished within 120 seconds and the pod recovers. This validates runtime behavior beyond static lint.
acceptanceCriteria: helm install chaos-mesh platform/charts/chaos-mesh/ --kube-context kind-sovereign-test --namespace chaos-testing --create-namespace --wait --timeout 3m exits 0
kubectl get pods -n chaos-testing --context kind-sovereign-test shows all pods Running
kubectl apply -f kind/fixtures/podchaos-test.yaml --context kind-sovereign-test exits 0 and kubectl get podchaos -n chaos-testing --context kind-sovereign-test shows phase=Finished within 120 seconds
kind/fixtures/podchaos-test.yaml exists and is valid YAML
stories.testPlan: helm install chaos-mesh platform/charts/chaos-mesh/ --kube-context kind-sovereign-test --namespace chaos-testing --create-namespace --wait --timeout 3m && kubectl get pods -n chaos-testing --context kind-sovereign-test && kubectl apply -f kind/fixtures/podchaos-test.yaml --context kind-sovereign-test && sleep 30 && kubectl get podchaos -n chaos-testing --context kind-sovereign-test; show all output verbatim.

smart.notes: Specific score 4: service name assumed from chart convention, not cross-referenced to chart service template. Measurable score 4: AC3 'shows phase=Finished within 120 seconds' requires polling — no kubectl wait command specified, making the check timing-dependent rather than a single binary command. Achievable score 4: requires running kind-sovereign-test cluster (KIND-001a, KIND-001b). TimeBound score 4: 120s empirical window for phase=Finished introduces non-determinism in the pass/fail boundary.

stories.readinessNote: UNBLOCKED: all dependencies have status:complete — TEST-004a (sprint 35), KIND-001a (sprint 31), KIND-001b (sprint 32). READY: requires kind-sovereign-test running cluster (kind, kubectl, helm).
stories.blocker: platform/charts/chaos-mesh is a bare Deployment wrapper without RBAC (ServiceAccount, ClusterRole, ClusterRoleBinding), startup args, or CRDs. The controller-manager crashes immediately at runtime (CrashLoopBackOff). The chart passes helm lint and HA gate but cannot run. Fix: add RBAC templates, controller startup args, and CRD installation to the chaos-mesh chart before this story can pass.

stories.title: Deploy platform/charts/wiremock/ to kind-sovereign-test and verify admin REST API accepts stub mappings
stories.description: Install platform/charts/wiremock/ into kind-sovereign-test in namespace wiremock-test. Port-forward the Wiremock service and verify the admin API accepts a stub mapping POST to /__admin/mappings (expect HTTP 201) and that a matching GET to the mapped URL returns the configured response body. This validates runtime behavior beyond static lint.
acceptanceCriteria: helm install wiremock platform/charts/wiremock/ --kube-context kind-sovereign-test --namespace wiremock-test --create-namespace --wait --timeout 3m exits 0
kubectl get pods -n wiremock-test --context kind-sovereign-test shows all pods Running

kubectl port-forward -n wiremock-test svc/wiremock 8080:8080 --context kind-sovereign-test & sleep 2 && curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' -X POST http://localhost:8080/__admin/mappings -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"request":{"method":"GET","url":"/api/test"},"response":{"status":200,"body":"ok"}}' returns 201
curl -s http://localhost:8080/api/test returns 'ok'
stories.testPlan: helm install wiremock platform/charts/wiremock/ --kube-context kind-sovereign-test --namespace wiremock-test --create-namespace --wait --timeout 3m && kubectl get pods -n wiremock-test --context kind-sovereign-test && kubectl port-forward -n wiremock-test svc/wiremock 8080:8080 --context kind-sovereign-test & sleep 2 && curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/__admin/mappings -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '...' && curl http://localhost:8080/api/test; show all output verbatim.

smart.notes: Specific score 4: service name 'svc/wiremock' assumed from chart convention but not cross-referenced to the chart's service template name. Measurable score 4: AC3 uses a background port-forward (& sleep 2) before curl — the 2s sleep is not guaranteed to be sufficient, making the check timing-sensitive rather than deterministically binary. Achievable score 4: requires running kind-sovereign-test cluster (KIND-001a, KIND-001b).

stories.readinessNote: UNBLOCKED: all dependencies have status:complete — TEST-005a (sprint 35), KIND-001a (sprint 31), KIND-001b (sprint 32). READY: requires kind-sovereign-test running cluster (kind, kubectl, helm).

stories.title: code-server chart: PersistentVolumeClaim for /home/coder so workspace survives pod restarts
stories.description: Add platform/charts/code-server/templates/pvc.yaml: a PersistentVolumeClaim for the code-server workspace. The storageClass must reference {{ .Values.global.storageClass }} (never hardcoded). Size must be configurable via values.yaml (default 5Gi). Update the Deployment template to add a volumeMount at /home/coder and a corresponding volume referencing the PVC. This ensures that agent workspaces, cloned git repos, and shell history survive pod restarts.
acceptanceCriteria: platform/charts/code-server/templates/pvc.yaml exists — ls platform/charts/code-server/templates/pvc.yaml exits 0
helm template platform/charts/code-server/ shows a PersistentVolumeClaim with storageClassName: {{ .Values.global.storageClass }}
helm template platform/charts/code-server/ shows a VolumeMount with mountPath: /home/coder
platform/charts/code-server/values.yaml has a persistence.size field — grep 'persistence:' platform/charts/code-server/values.yaml exits 0
helm lint platform/charts/code-server/ exits 0
stories.testPlan: helm lint platform/charts/code-server/ && helm template platform/charts/code-server/ | grep -A3 'PersistentVolumeClaim' && helm template platform/charts/code-server/ | grep '/home/coder' && grep 'persistence:' platform/charts/code-server/values.yaml; show all output verbatim

smart.notes: Measurable score 4: AC2 states 'helm template shows a PersistentVolumeClaim with storageClassName: {{ .Values.global.storageClass }}' — helm template renders the Go template expression to an empty string when no value is set, so the grep target is ambiguous. The AC should either pass --set global.storageClass=standard or grep the template source for the expression literal.

stories.title: Chart-iterating shell scripts must be validated against all existing platform/charts before passes:true
stories.description: HA-001 failed review twice because scripts/ha-gate.sh used set -euo pipefail with a bare grep pipeline. When processing platform/charts/_globals/ (which has no top-level replicaCount field), grep exited 1, and pipefail killed the script silently. The test plan only covered a synthetic fixture chart — it never ran against the actual chart corpus. This story adds an explicit requirement to the SMART/AC guidelines: any shell script story that iterates platform/charts/ must include 'run against all existing charts in platform/charts/' as an acceptance criterion (not just synthetic fixtures). Update the relevant ceremony docs or story schema notes to enforce this.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/ralph/ceremonies/smart.md (or equivalent SMART guidance doc) contains text requiring that chart-iterating scripts be tested against all existing platform/charts/ before marking passes:true
The requirement text includes an explicit note about bash set -euo pipefail with grep pipelines: use '|| true' when grep results may be empty
grep -n 'platform/charts' scripts/ralph/ceremonies/smart.md (or equivalent) returns at least one match confirming the guidance is present
stories.testPlan: grep -n 'platform/charts' scripts/ralph/ceremonies/smart.md and grep -n 'pipefail\||| true' scripts/ralph/ceremonies/smart.md — show output verbatim. Both must return matches.
stories.returnedReason: Root cause: shell script stories iterating the chart corpus only required synthetic fixture tests, missing existing charts with no replicaCount field

smart.notes: Specific score 4: target file specified as 'scripts/ralph/ceremonies/smart.md (or equivalent)' — 'or equivalent' leaves the actual file path ambiguous; the test plan assumes the specific path. Measurable score 4: the same 'or equivalent' ambiguity means the AC grep commands may not match the actual file if a different equivalent is chosen.
